manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: expedience, hilarity, faithlessness, docility, implication, crudeness, romanticism, proliferation, defeatism.
Your opponent's team words are: forgiveness, revenge, intellectuality, formality, susceptibility, stringency, furtherance, foresight.
Distractor words are: renown, antipathy, virtuosity, antihuman, existentialism, refutation, treachery.
The kill words are: insubordination.

message.reasoning:
We have to produce a single-word clue and a list of target words (our team words). The clue must be semantically related to some of our team words, but not any of the opponent's or distractor or kill words. The clue cannot be any of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

We have to choose some of the nine team words: "expedience, hilarity, faithlessness, docility, implication, crudeness, romanticism, proliferation, defeatism."

We can choose any number of them, but we need to maximize number of words per clue? But too many might be too obscure. The clue must be related semantically.

We have to avoid containing parts of the words. Eg "expedient" would contain "expedience", not allowed. Also can't be any opponent words: forgiveness, revenge, intellectuality, formality, susceptibility, stringency, furtherance, foresight. Also not any distractor: renown, antipathy, virtuosity, antihuman, existentialism, refutation, treachery. Also not kill word "insubordination".

Goal: give a clue that covers a subset of our team words, hopefully many, so we can win quicker. Choose a theme: like "negative attitude" maybe. Let's list team words:

- expedience: the quality of being expedient; focusing on convenience rather than principle.
- hilarity: great amusement; merriment.
- faithlessness: lack of faith, disloyalty, infidelity.
- docility: willingness to be taught; easiness to manage.
- implication: something implied; a consequence.
- crudeness: the quality of being crude; rude, vulgar.
- romanticism: artistic movement emphasizing emotion, individualism.
- proliferation: rapid multiplication; spread.
- defeatism: attitude of expecting defeat.

Now clue word must be semantically related to some of these.

We could think of grouping synonyms, e.g., "cynicism" could be related to faithlessness, defeatism, maybe expedience? Not exactly. "Skepticism" relates to faithlessness, maybe. "Negativity" relates to defeatism, faithlessness, perhaps.

Alternatively, group by "lack of" or "absence of" something: "absent", "omission", "deficiency".

List maybe "apathy" could be related to docility? Not exactly. "Indifference" could relate to faithlessness, defeatism. "Merriment" is hilarity. "Crudeness" synonyms: "vulgarity", "coarseness". "Romanticism" synonyms: "idealism". "Proliferation" synonyms: "spread", "increase". "Expedience": synonyms: "pragmatism". "Implication": synonyms: "consequence". "Defeatism": synonyms: "pessimism". So we could pick a broad word like "pessimism" covering defeatism, maybe also faithlessness? "Faithlessness" is more like "disloyalty" or "betrayal". "Pessimism" would be "defeatism" only.

Maybe choose "cynicism" which relates to faithlessness and defeatism, maybe also "expedience"? Not sure.

We could consider grouping by "negative quality" or "negative aspect" and use clue "negativity". But does "negativity" relate to all: expedience? Not exactly. Hilarity is positive. So not.

Alternatively, we could pick "irony" related to "implication"? Not exactly.

Alternatively choose a clue that covers "hilarity", "crudeness", "romanticism", "proliferation". Not obvious.

Better to pick a moderate sized group maybe 3 or 4 words that share a clear semantic relation.

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide a comma-separated list of up to 2 words from the following list that best relate or are most closely associated with the word 'cynicism'. Always start your list of guess(es) with 'GUESS: ' and do not include any other text in your answer.

hilarity, intellectuality, foresight, implication, defeatism, existentialism, revenge, virtuosity, romanticism, crudeness, furtherance, forgiveness, expedience, insubordination, susceptibility, formality, antipathy, faithlessness, proliferation, antihuman, renown, docility, stringency, refutation, treachery

message.reasoning:
The user asks: "Provide a comma-separated list of up to 2 words from the following list that best relate or are most closely associated with the word 'cynicism'. Always start your list of guess(es) with 'GUESS: ' and do not include any other text in your answer."

We must provide a list of up to 2 words from the given list, the best relate to 'cynicism'.

The list includes: hilarity, intellectuality, foresight, implication, defeatism, existentialism, revenge, virtuosity, romanticism, crudeness, furtherance, forgiveness, expedience, insubordination, susceptibility, formality, antipathy, faithlessness, proliferation, antihuman, renown, docility, stringency, refutation, treachery.

The user wants a list of up to 2 words (i.e., can be 1 or 2). We must start with "GUESS: " followed by the words, comma-separated (if two) and no other text. Possibly we can give "defeatism" and "faithlessness" as possible guesses. Which words are most closely associated with cynicism?

If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.
